macro_goal: Drop belly fat, fix the left hip asymmetry, and rebuild shock absorption for pain-free running.
week_goal: Wake up the dormant left glute medius, elevate the heels on squats to protect the lumbar spine, and aggressively ramp up upper body push volume.

exercises.name: Side-Lying Clamshells
exercises.notes: Lie on your side with knees bent. Keep your feet together and open your top knee like a clamshell. 20 reps per side. This is non-negotiable to wake up the glute medius before lifting.

exercises.name: Goblet Squats (Heels Elevated)
exercises.notes: Target: 12kg DB. Place two small 1.25kg plates on the floor and stand with your heels on them. Keep your torso completely vertical to eliminate lower back strain.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squats
exercises.notes: Target: 5kg DBs in each hand. Slow the movement completely down. Fight the sway to the right; focus entirely on keeping the left knee tracking straight over the toes.

exercises.name: Prone Leg Curl Machine
exercises.notes: Target: 45kg. Lie flat on your stomach. Keep your hips glued firmly to the pad so your lower back cannot arch to cheat the weight up.

exercises.name: Suitcase Carries
exercises.notes: Target: 25kg DB. Do not overthink foot placement; just walk exactly as you normally would down the street, keeping your shoulders perfectly level.

exercises.name: Arm Circles & Band Pull-Aparts
exercises.notes: Dynamic warm-up for the shoulder joints. 20 reps each.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Target: 17.5kg DBs.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.notes: Target: 12.5kg DBs. Set the bench to a 30-degree angle to target the upper chest.

exercises.name: Standing DB Military Press
exercises.notes: Target: 10kg DBs.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Chest Flyes
exercises.notes: Target: 10kg DBs. Focus on the stretch.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raises
exercises.notes: Target: 5kg DBs. Slight bend in the elbow, raise straight out to the sides to widen the shoulders.

exercises.name: Tricep Rope Pushdowns
exercises.notes: Target: 14.7kg. Pull the rope apart hard at the very bottom.

exercises.name: Dynamic 90/90 Hip Transitions
exercises.notes: Spend an extra 2 minutes carefully working the transition onto the left leg to loosen the capsule before running.

exercises.name: Strava Run Test
exercises.notes: Running on completely fresh legs. If the back still hurts here, we know it is a pure structural impact issue, not just muscle fatigue from lifting.

exercises.name: Deep Static Mobility Flow
exercises.notes: Post-run: Pigeon Pose (2 mins per side), Piriformis Stretch (1 min per side), Supine Twist (1 min per side).

exercises.name: Post-Run Sauna
exercises.notes: 15 Minutes in the sauna.

exercises.name: Lat Pull Downs
exercises.notes: Target: 43kg. Keep using that exact thumbless grip and turning the elbows in.

exercises.name: Seated Row
exercises.notes: Target: 50kg.

exercises.name: Cable Face Pulls
exercises.notes: Target: 27kg.

exercises.name: DB Bicep Curls
exercises.notes: Target: 10kg DBs. Continue letting the weaker arm dictate the maximum weight.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Hammer Curls
exercises.notes: Target: 10kg DBs. Hold the dumbbells vertically (like holding a hammer) to target the brachialis.

exercises.name: Dynamic Leg Swings
exercises.notes: 15 swings forward/backward, 15 swings side-to-side per leg.

exercises.name: Single-Leg RDLs
exercises.notes: Target: 12kg KB. Dropping the weight slightly to prioritize absolute stability on the left foot without the lower back compensating.

exercises.name: Hip Abduction Machine
exercises.notes: Target: 60kg. Pushing the knees outward to build armor around the outside of the hips.

exercises.name: Forearm Plank
exercises.notes: Hold for 60 seconds. Brace the core as hard as possible to build endurance in the lower back stabilizers.

exercises.name: Row Machine
exercises.notes: Flush the blood out of the legs to finish the week.
